person_name: Aureolus, commander of Gallienus' cavalry and usurper (d. 268)
person_name_full: Aureolus, commander of Gallienus' cavalry and usurper (d. 268)
notes: Commander of Gallienus' cavalry and usurper who rebelled during the Crisis of the Third Century.

docs.title: Life of Claudius
docs.author: Historia Augusta (anonymous)
docs.excerpt: … against Aureolus,⁠ 20 who was the more dangerous to the commonwealth because he had found great favour with Gallienus, he thrust him from the helm of the state; then he pronounced him a pretender, sending proclamations to the people and also despatching messages to the senate.

docs.title: Life of Tyranni Triginta
docs.author: Historia Augusta (anonymous)
docs.excerpt: … after trying in vain to conquer so brave a man and being now on the point of beginning a war against Postumus, made peace with him " of which events many have already been related and many are still to be told.
